thought_beat_text: He's standing at the edge where the overhead light dies and the dark begins, and he can see the foam-core lip of the set like a wound he wasn't supposed to find—the whole thing is a construction and he knew it, but knowing it and seeing the seam are two different kinds of damage. What he can't work out is whether the performance was ever sincere or whether sincerity was just another layer of the set, and the question won't stay still long enough to answer.
residue_summary: The seam between the lit stage and the backstage dark has become the only honest place he can locate, and he is not sure he can walk back into the light without pretending he didn't see it.
image_hint: a figure at the threshold where stage light ends and raw darkness begins, one hand touching the exposed cardboard edge
audio_hint: a single overhead fluorescent flicker, then held silence with low ambient hum
